_meta.name: HODIE Clinical Library v4.2
_meta.description: Australia-Optimised, Longevity-Focused biomarker reference ranges — converted from YAML v4.1 spec

apob.name: ApoB
interpretationNotes: Prioritise over LDL-C when discordant.

lpa.name: Lipoprotein(a)
actionDomains: Consider specialist referral if very high
interpretationNotes: Largely genetic; lifestyle has limited impact.

hscrp.name: hs-CRP
interpretationNotes: Interpret away from acute infection or trauma.

tc.name: Total Cholesterol
interpretationNotes: Interpret with ApoB/LDL-C/HDL-C/TG.

ldl.name: LDL-C
interpretationNotes: ApoB preferred when discordant; LDL-C can underestimate particle burden.

hdl.name: HDL-C
interpretationNotes: Focus on TG/insulin/ApoB rather than trying to 'raise HDL' directly.

tg.name: Triglycerides
interpretationNotes: Best interpreted fasting; elevated TG often tracks insulin resistance.

apobapoa1.name: ApoB/ApoA1 ratio
actionDomains: Lower ApoB via lifestyle and/or medication
interpretationNotes: Derived from ApoB + ApoA1; no extra lab cost.

hcy.name: Homocysteine
interpretationNotes: Interpret alongside B12/folate and renal function.

cpep.name: C-peptide
interpretationNotes: Useful adjunct when fasting insulin is equivocal; interpret with glucose/insulin.
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

ast.name: AST
interpretationNotes: Interpret with ALT/GGT and clinical context.

alp.name: ALP
interpretationNotes: Interpret with bilirubin, GGT and calcium/phosphate if bone suspected.
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

bili.name: Bilirubin (total)
interpretationNotes: Mild isolated elevation may reflect Gilbert’s; interpret with ALT/ALP/GGT.

alb.name: Albumin
interpretationNotes: Low albumin is a strong general risk signal; interpret contextually.

creat.name: Creatinine
interpretationNotes: Interpret with eGFR and muscle mass.
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

urea.name: Urea
actionDomains: Review protein intake if very high

na.name: Sodium
interpretationNotes: Interpret in clinical context and with medications (e.g., diuretics).
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

k.name: Potassium
interpretationNotes: Interpret in clinical context and with medications (e.g., diuretics).
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

cl.name: Chloride
interpretationNotes: Interpret in clinical context and with medications (e.g., diuretics).
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

hco3.name: Bicarbonate
interpretationNotes: Interpret in clinical context and with medications (e.g., diuretics).
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

ca.name: Calcium
interpretationNotes: Interpret in clinical context and with medications (e.g., diuretics).
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

po4.name: Phosphate
interpretationNotes: Interpret in clinical context and with medications (e.g., diuretics).
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

ferritin.name: Ferritin
actionDomains: Iron optimisation guided by full iron studies
interpretationNotes: Interpret with transferrin saturation and CRP.

iron.name: Serum iron
interpretationNotes: Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

transferrin.name: Transferrin
interpretationNotes: Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

tsat.name: Transferrin saturation
actionDomains: Iron optimisation; clinical review if persistently abnormal

rbc.name: Red blood cell count
interpretationNotes: Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

wbc.name: White blood cell count
actionDomains: Clinical review if abnormal; interpret with differential
interpretationNotes: Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

plt.name: Platelets
interpretationNotes: Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

hct.name: Haematocrit
actionDomains: Interpret with Hb/RBC and hydration status

mcv.name: MCV
interpretationNotes: Macrocytosis strongly suggests B12/folate issues or alcohol/thyroid context.

mch.name: MCH
interpretationNotes: Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

rdw.name: RDW
interpretationNotes: RDW is a strong general risk marker; interpret alongside CBC and nutrition markers.

testosteronem.name: Testosterone (total)
interpretationNotes: Morning test preferred; interpret with SHBG.  Free testosterone can be calculated.

estradiolf.name: Estradiol (E2)
interpretationNotes: Interpret with cycle phase and menopausal status; interpret alongside SHBG where relevant.

shbg.name: SHBG
interpretationNotes: Required to interpret testosterone accurately.

tsh.name: TSH
interpretationNotes: Interpret with Free T4/Free T3 and symptoms.

ft4.name: Free T4
interpretationNotes: Interpret with TSH and Free T3.

ft3.name: Free T3
interpretationNotes: Interpret with TSH and Free T4.

vitd.name: Vitamin D (25-OH)
interpretationNotes: Longevity optimal range is typically higher than minimum deficiency threshold.

b12.name: Vitamin B12
interpretationNotes: Interpret with folate, MCV and homocysteine.

folate.name: Folate
interpretationNotes: Interpret with B12 (do not treat folate alone if B12 deficient).

mg.name: Magnesium
interpretationNotes: Serum Mg is imperfect; still useful screening marker.

zn.name: Zinc
interpretationNotes: Interpret alongside copper; aim for balance rather than extremes.

lppla2.name: Lp-PLA2
interpretationNotes: Interpret with ApoB, hs-CRP and overall risk profile.
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

oxldl.name: Oxidised LDL (OxLDL)
interpretationNotes: Use as an adjunct marker; interpret trend over time.
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

apoc3.name: Apolipoprotein C-III (ApoC-III)
interpretationNotes: Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

il6.name: Interleukin-6 (IL-6)
interpretationNotes: Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

tnf.name: TNF-alpha
actionDomains: As per IL-6; investigate drivers if high
interpretationNotes: Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

esr.name: ESR
actionDomains: Interpret with hs-CRP; clinical review if persistently elevated
interpretationNotes: Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

cortisolam.name: Cortisol (AM)
interpretationNotes: Time of day critical; interpret with symptoms and sleep data.
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

dheas.name: DHEA-S
interpretationNotes: Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

igf1.name: IGF-1
interpretationNotes: Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

leptin.name: Leptin
interpretationNotes: Interpret with BMI/body fat and insulin markers.

omega3.name: Omega-3 Index
interpretationNotes: Highly actionable; retest after 3–6 months if actively correcting.

copper.name: Copper
interpretationNotes: Consider zinc:copper balance rather than single value.

stfr.name: Soluble Transferrin Receptor (sTfR)
actionDomains: Iron optimisation guided by full iron picture
interpretationNotes: Complements ferritin; useful when inflammation confounds ferritin.
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

psa.name: PSA
interpretationNotes: Optional; requires informed consent and appropriate follow-up pathway.
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

ca125.name: CA-125
actionDomains: Clinical discussion and follow-up if elevated
interpretationNotes: Optional; non-specific; interpret with symptoms and clinical pathway.
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

globulin.name: Globulin
interpretationNotes: Often derived from total protein and albumin.
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

agratio.name: Albumin/Globulin ratio
interpretationNotes: Derived metric.

ldh.name: LDH
interpretationNotes: Non-specific; use as context marker.
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

nonhdl.name: Non-HDL cholesterol
interpretationNotes: Derived: TC - HDL.

remnant.name: Remnant cholesterol
interpretationNotes: Derived; formula depends on available lipids.

tghdl.name: TG/HDL ratio
interpretationNotes: Derived; interpret with insulin/HbA1c.

homair.name: HOMA-IR
interpretationNotes: Derived from fasting glucose + insulin.

insgluratio.name: Insulin:Glucose ratio
interpretationNotes: Derived metric.

astaltratio.name: AST/ALT ratio
interpretationNotes: Derived metric; interpret clinically.

neut.name: Neutrophils (abs)
interpretationNotes: Part of CBC differential.
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

lymph.name: Lymphocytes (abs)
interpretationNotes: Part of CBC differential.
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

mono.name: Monocytes (abs)
interpretationNotes: Part of CBC differential.
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

eos.name: Eosinophils (abs)
interpretationNotes: Part of CBC differential.
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

baso.name: Basophils (abs)
interpretationNotes: Part of CBC differential.
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

nlr.name: Neutrophil/Lymphocyte ratio
interpretationNotes: Derived from CBC differential.

uacr.name: Urine albumin/creatinine ratio (uACR)
interpretationNotes: High value early marker; repeat to confirm elevation.

urinealb.name: Urine albumin
interpretationNotes: Usually used as part of uACR.
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

urinecreat.name: Urine creatinine
interpretationNotes: Part of uACR calculation.
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

vita.name: Vitamin A (retinol)
interpretationNotes: Optional supporting nutrient marker.

vite.name: Vitamin E (alpha-tocopherol)
interpretationNotes: Optional supporting nutrient marker.

selenium.name: Selenium
interpretationNotes: Optional supporting nutrient marker.

crpstd.name: CRP (standard)
interpretationNotes: Included as context; hs-CRP is primary.

ck.name: Creatine kinase (CK)
interpretationNotes: Useful in performance users.
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

faratio.name: Fatty acid ratio (Omega-6:3)
interpretationNotes: Often derived when fatty acids measured.

b12active.name: Active B12 (holotranscobalamin)
interpretationNotes: Optional refinement; include only if available/cost-effective.
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

hcycontext.name: Methylmalonic acid (MMA)
interpretationNotes: Optional; improves specificity for B12 deficiency.
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

lipase.name: Lipase
interpretationNotes: Optional.
Interpret using the reporting lab’s reference interval and clinical context; trend over time is often more informative than a single value.

ferritinindex.name: Ferritin index (sTfR/log ferritin)
interpretationNotes: Derived from sTfR and ferritin.

core.description: Weighted composite of cardiovascular risk, metabolic health, inflammation, hormonal balance, organ resilience, and body composition

elite.description: Blind spot detection + aging precision overlay on top of CORE score
